name: Gulf Trust
description_short: Gulf Trust Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, non-profit credit union serving healthcare employees and select groups in Mississippi since 1972, offering NCUA-insured accounts and financial products.
description_long:
Gulf Trust Federal Credit Union was established in 1972 as the J C Hospital System Employees Credit Union by a group of employees from Singing River Hospital and Ocean Springs Hospital in Mississippi. Originally chartered to provide economic savings and loans to hospital co-workers and their families, the institution operated from within hospital facilities for its first three decades. The credit union underwent significant expansion, growing from its initial membership base to $6.8 million in assets by 1995, and achieved complete financial independence in 2003 by purchasing its own building at 2533 Denny Ave in Pascagoula, MS. In 2015, members voted to adopt the simpler name "Gulf Trust Credit Union" to better reflect the organization's locality and financial strength.

Gulf Trust offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services typical of a full-service credit union. Members have access to various deposit accounts, loans (consumer and otherwise), and online banking with mobile app functionality. The credit union displays current rates on its website and provides financial calculators to assist members with planning. Account holders benefit from NCUA insurance protection, and the institution maintains a physical presence with drive-up services and ATM access. The routing number 265377976 is provided for electronic transactions.

What distinguishes Gulf Trust is its specialized membership base and deep institutional roots. Rather than serving the general public, it primarily serves employees and families of the Singing River Health System and other select employee groups (SEGs) within the healthcare industry. This focused approach allows the credit union to tailor products and services to the specific financial needs of healthcare workers. The organization is governed democratically through member-elected boards and committees composed entirely of unpaid volunteers, embodying the credit union philosophy of "People Helping People." The institution is regulated under the Federal Credit Union Act and maintains both governance and supervisory oversight structures.

Gulf Trust is a legitimate, NCUA-insured financial institution with over 50 years of operational history and transparent governance. The main caveat for prospective members is that membership eligibility is restricted to employees of Singing River Health System and certain select employee groups in healthcare—it is not open to the general public. The website provides essential operational information but lacks detailed product descriptions, rate specifics, or comprehensive fee schedules, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly for detailed information. The institution appears well-established and stable, though its small size relative to larger credit unions may limit the breadth of advanced financial products.
services: Share accounts (savings accounts) and deposit products
Online banking platform with login access
Mobile banking apps (iOS App Store and Google Play)
pros: NCUA-insured accounts providing federal protection up to standard limits
Online banking and mobile app access for convenient account management
Established 50+ year operational history with demonstrated financial stability and growth
Physical location with drive-up and ATM services at 2533 Denny Ave, Pascagoula
Non-profit structure with democratic member governance and unpaid volunteer leadership
Specialized focus on healthcare employee financial needs allows tailored services
Full-service financial institution offering multiple product categories (accounts, loans, calculators)
cons: Membership is restricted to Singing River Health System employees and select healthcare employee groups—not open to general public
Website lacks specific details on loan rates, APRs, account fees, and product terms, requiring direct contact
Limited information about specific loan types, maximum amounts, or qualification requirements
Smaller credit union with potentially fewer advanced products compared to larger financial institutions
Minimal digital presence regarding community service details, disaster preparedness programs, or member testimonials
best_for: Employees of Singing River Health System seeking member-owned financial services with democratic governance
Healthcare industry workers in Mississippi eligible for select employee group (SEG) membership
Members prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and community-oriented credit union philosophy
Individuals seeking personalized service from a locally-rooted, non-profit financial institution
diagnosis: Gulf Trust is best for employees of Singing River Health System and eligible healthcare workers in Mississippi seeking NCUA-protected accounts and member-owned financial services with transparent governance. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this credit union is not open to the general public and requires employment affiliation with specific healthcare organizations.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
